solution:
def earliestAndLatest(n, firstPlayer, secondPlayer):
    rounds = [0] * (n - 1)
    rounds[firstPlayer - 1] = firstPlayer
    rounds[secondPlayer - 1] = secondPlayer

    result = [rounds[firstPlayer - 1], rounds[secondPlayer - 1]]
    for i in range(1, n):
        if rounds[i - 1] == rounds[i - 1]:
            rounds[i - 1] = i
            result.append(i)
    return result

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
We initialize a list of n-1 elements to store the round numbers for each player. Then, we set the firstPlayer and secondPlayer as the first and second elements of the list, respectively. Next, we create a list result to store the round numbers of the players who will compete against each other. We iterate from round 2 to n-1 and check if the players at the same index in rounds list are equal. If they are equal, we update their round numbers and append them to the result list. Finally, we return the result list containing the round numbers of the players who will compete against each other.
